title: rosa caesia subsp. bakeri
intro: Rosa caesia subsp. bakeri, also known as the Himalayan rose or Baker's rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its fragrant, white to pale pink flowers and thorny stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa caesia subsp. bakeri are believed to include wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of various skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often made by combining the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots with other medicinal herbs.
